subject: GRB 240417A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

At 18:46:10 UT on 17 Apr 2024, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 240417A (trigger 735072375.64676 / 240417782).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 30.7, Dec = -49.7 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 02h 02m, -49d 42'), with a statistical uncertainty of 12.8 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 133.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240417782/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn240417782.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240417782/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn240417782.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240417782/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn240417782.gif
